slides.title: Stillness is *training* for your brain.
slides.sub: A few quiet minutes a day can calm the stress response that quietly ages you. Here is what it does.

slides.title: Stress wears the body down
slides.body: Chronic stress keeps cortisol high and the alarm centers of the brain on edge. Over years, that *speeds up cellular aging*.

slides.title: A calmer alarm system
slides.body: Regular mindfulness practice is linked to a less reactive amygdala, the brain's threat detector. The volume on stress *turns down*.

slides.title: Lower cortisol output
slides.body: Steady practice may help blunt the cortisol your body releases under pressure, easing the load on your *brain and immune system*.

slides.title: Protecting your cells
slides.body: Less stress signaling can support the cellular repair that keeps you sharp. It is one of the gentlest longevity habits there is.

slides.title: Small and consistent wins
slides.body: Five to ten quiet minutes most days beats one long session. *Consistency* is what trains the nervous system to settle.

slides.title: Make calm a *daily habit*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your sleep, food, movement and steps in one daily number, so the calm you build shows up in how you actually feel.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your brain can be trained to handle stress better. A few quiet minutes a day is the workout. 🧘‍♀️🌿

Mindfulness is linked to a calmer amygdala and lower cortisol, easing the stress that quietly ages your brain and immune system. Save this. 🔖
